title: Shadow Accounting in Insurance Compensation
title_original: Shadow Accounting in Insurance Compensation
summary: Why shadow accounting happens in insurance Insurance compensation is uniquely difficult to manage because it sits at the intersection of money, relationships, compliance, and strategy. But in sales and compensation environments, shadow accounting usually looks less formal, consisting of spreadsheets, offline trackers, manually maintained commission logs, and side calculations used to validate or challenge official payout information. In insurance distribution, shadow accounting often appears when producers, managers, or back-office teams cannot easily see how commissions were calculated, whether adjustments were applied, or how hierarchy and eligibility changes affected payment.

title: Despite softening, reinsurance pricing at 2021/2022 levels “widely viewed as adequate” – KBW
title_original: Despite softening, reinsurance pricing at 2021/2022 levels “widely viewed as adequate” – KBW
summary: In general, analysts from KBW heard during a recent Bermuda trip that reinsurance pricing is down about 20% at the mid-year renewals (the report is headlined “20% is the new 15%”), but even after the softening taking rates back to levels seen in 2021 to 2022, this is still “widely viewed as adequate”, the analysis explains. Summing up on pricing that, “We heard several comparisons of this year’s pricing to 2021/2022 levels, which is still widely viewed as adequate and a far cry from the low point of 2017.” Terms and conditions are seen as “mostly holding”, albeit with some concessions that improve coverage for reinsurance buyers. KBW’s analysts said, “Most reinsurers appear far more willing to accept lower pricing than to yield changes on the much improved T&C changes that were achieved at the 1/1/2023 onset of the recent hardening cycle.” But, the analysts cite a “gamut of minor T&C changes”, such: as minimum premium true-ups to exposure now being allowed to float down and up; Florida reinsurance premiums now being paid quarterly instead of upfront; broadening of retrocession covers to named natural perils, from named specific perils (this is one to watch if events happen); aggregates, cascading covers, and top and drops now being more widely available; plus some modest extension of hours clauses and radius definitions (again, ones to watch when events occur).

title: US P&C insurance industry faces weaker growth in 2026 despite improved underwriting results
title_original: US P&C insurance industry faces weaker growth in 2026 despite improved underwriting results
summary: The US property and casualty (P&C) insurance industry is projected to see underlying growth fall to -3.7% in the first half of 2026, down from 1.6% in 2025, as insurers continue to contend with catastrophe exposure, inflationary pressures and rising claims costs, according to new forecasts from the Insurance Information Institute (Triple-I) and actuarial and consulting firm Milliman. Despite the more difficult outlook for 2026, the industry recorded stronger underwriting performance in 2025. The organisations also reported improved homeowners insurance results despite ongoing catastrophe activity, including the Los Angeles wildfires earlier in the year.
why_matters: Impacto directo en exposición, pricing y reservas del sector.

title: Dominick Hoare to retire at year-end after more than 32 years with Munich Re
title_original: Dominick Hoare to retire at year-end after more than 32 years with Munich Re
summary: Dominick Hoare, Group Chief Underwriting Officer, Munich Re Specialty, Global Markets and Active Underwriting Syndicate 457, is set to retire at the end of 2026 after more than 32 years of service. John Wilkinson, CEO of Munich Re Specialty, Global Markets, said: “Dominick has made an exceptional contribution to Munich Re over more than three decades. Hoare joined Munich Re, one of Europe’s big four reinsurance companies, in 1994 and has held various senior underwriting and leadership positions during his tenure.
why_matters: Los cambios de liderazgo suelen anticipar cambios estratégicos.

title: Inszone Acquires Florida’s Coastal Insurance, Shepherd Moves to New Bradenton Office
title_original: Inszone Acquires Florida’s Coastal Insurance, Shepherd Moves to New Bradenton Office
summary: Also in Florida, Shepherd Insurance announced it is moving its office from Sarasota to Pendery Place in the Bradenton/Lakewood Ranch area. California-headquartered Inszone Insurance Services has continued its hot streak of acquisitions with the purchase of Coastal Insurance Services in Florida and affiliated Optimal Insurance Solutions. The Sarasota office saw the coming together of five agencies over the past 13 years through acquisitions, making the Bradenton move a natural next step in the firm’s long-term growth plan for the Florida market, Shepherd leadership said.
why_matters: Consolida el mercado y puede redefinir la competencia en el sector.

title: Business Moves: Vienna Insurance Purchases Nuernberger; Liberty Mutual Raises Stake in India Unit; Aon’s NFP Buys Irish Private Wealth Specialist; Gallagher Buys UK Law Firm
title_original: Business Moves: Vienna Insurance Purchases Nuernberger; Liberty Mutual Raises Stake in India Unit; Aon’s NFP Buys Irish Private Wealth Specialist; Gallagher Buys UK Law Firm
summary: Liberty Mutual Raises Stake in India Unit to 74% Vienna Insurance Group Completes Purchase of Germany’s Nuernberger Vienna Insurance Group (VIG) has completed its acquisition of Nuernberger Beteiligungs AG for 1.38 billion euros ($1.6 billion), in VIG’s largest-ever transaction. “As part of Vienna Insurance Group, we will significantly accelerate our transformation into a prevention insurer — a strong positioning from which our customers, our sales partners and our employees will benefit,” according to Harald Rosenberger, CEO of Nuernberger.

title: Michigan Secures $108M Settlement with Monsanto for PCB Contamination
title_original: Michigan Secures $108M Settlement with Monsanto for PCB Contamination
summary: Michigan Attorney General Dana Nessel has secured a $108 million settlement with Monsanto Company (PDF) , the major manufacturer of polychlorinated biphenyls (PCBs), and affiliated companies Solutia Inc. and Pharmacia LLC to address harms from PCB contamination in the State of Michigan. The Michigan Department of Environment, Great Lakes, and Energy (EGLE) and the Michigan Department of Natural Resources (DNR) will jointly oversee the use of funds to remediate PCB-contaminated property and resources and restore natural resources adversely impacted by contamination.
why_matters: Nueva financiación que puede acelerar la competencia en el sector.
